channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rising aggression against ACT healthcare workers sparks calls for zero tolerance
item.description: A growing trend of violent incidents against healthcare workers in the Australian Capital Territory has prompted calls for stricter measures to protect staff. Dr. Sam Scanlan, an emergency medicine sp...

item.title: Meta Adds Parental Controls to Threads to Enhance Teen Safety
item.description: Meta has introduced parental supervision tools for Threads, allowing parents and guardians to monitor their teens' activity on the platform. The new features include tracking daily usage time, setting...

item.title: Key Developments in Philippine Politics and Legal Matters
item.description: The article highlights several critical developments in Philippine politics and legal affairs. Vice President Sara Duterte faces an impeachment trial, with the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) s...

item.title: NBA Elects First Female President Amid Controversy, Tinubu Commends Her Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) has elected Mrs. Badejo-Okusanya as its first female president, a historic milestone in the legal profession. President Tinubu praised her victory, highlighting her ...

item.title: Canada must adopt an aggressive strategy to counter Trump's tariff threats
item.description: The article critiques Canada's defensive approach to U.S. tariffs imposed by Donald Trump, which target Canadian goods worth $20 billion. It argues that retaliatory measures and a lack of strategic cl...

item.title: Kumba reports 43% drop in earnings due to record rainfall and reduced output
item.description: Kumba Iron Ore, a subsidiary of Anglo American PLC, reported a significant decline in headline earnings for the six months ended June 2026, with projections indicating a potential decrease of up to R4...

item.title: Government officials to relocate to Manchester as No 10 North initiative launches
item.description: Civil servants from London-based government departments have been offered relocation packages to move to the North of England, particularly Manchester, as part of the new No 10 North initiative. This ...

item.title: Teen faces animal cruelty charges after fatal cat attack in Winnipeg
item.description: A 16-year-old teenager is facing multiple animal cruelty charges and weapon possession allegations after allegedly fatally injuring a cat in Winnipeg. According to police reports, the incident occurre...

item.title: Hosepipe Bans and Drought Concerns in Southern England
item.description: Millions of households across southern England and parts of Wales face hosepipe bans due to prolonged extreme heat and reduced rainfall. Water companies like Thames Water have imposed restrictions as ...

item.title: BMX racing world championships held in Brisbane with Australian athletes on podium
item.description: The BMX racing world championships took place in Brisbane, Australia, bringing together over 3,000 riders from 41 nations for an intense competition that showcases speed, skill, and strategic riding. ...

item.title: Namibian study shows that using children's home languages improves English reading and learning
item.description: A recent study carried out in Namibia has found that children learn English more effectively when teachers include their home languages during reading lessons instead of relying on English alone. The ...

item.title: Liberation of Dachau Concentration Camp and Its Historical Significance
item.description: The article recounts the liberation of Dachau concentration camp in April 1945 by U.S. forces, highlighting the harrowing conditions endured by prisoners under Nazi rule. Dachau, established in 1933 a...

item.title: Odisha CM demands accountability for student protests and exam leaks
item.description: Former Odisha Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik has demanded the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, citing severe examination irregularities and violent attacks on protesting stud...

item.title: Nigeria opposition challenges Tinubu's leadership in Washington
item.description: The article discusses the Nigerian opposition's strategy to challenge President Tinubu's administration by seeking international support in Washington. It highlights Governor Seyi Makinde's appeal to ...

item.title: Google Launches Three New Gemini AI Models Focused on Efficiency and Cybersecurity
item.description: Google DeepMind has released three new Gemini models—Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber—emphasizing efficiency, cost reduction, and specialized capabilities. The 3.6 Flash model is ...

item.title: Roadworks Cause Significant Congestion for Jakarta-Tangerang Toll Road Commuters
item.description: The Jakarta-Tangerang toll road, a critical artery connecting Jakarta to Banten and key gateways, has faced persistent roadworks that have drastically increased travel times for commuters. Residents l...

item.title: Ridon Refutes Claims of Gossip Regarding NBI Chief's Statements
item.description: The article discusses the denial by Ridon, a prominent figure, of claims that the NBI chief's statements were labeled as 'tsismis' (gossip). The context involves an impeachment trial related to Sara D...

item.title: Oscar Sudi Discusses Ruto's 2022 VP Choice and Internal Party Dynamics
item.description: Kapseret MP Oscar Sudi has revealed that William Ruto's 2022 presidential victory hinged on selecting Rigathi Gachagua as his running mate over Kithure Kindiki. Despite internal resistance within Ruto...

item.title: Land Officer and Wife Attack in Kilombero District
item.description: Authorities in Tanzania’s Kilombero district are investigating the murder of a local land officer whose body was discovered after unknown assailants attacked him and seriously injured his wife in a vi...

item.title: US to impose new tariffs on countries over forced labor concerns
item.description: The US is set to introduce new tariffs targeting 60 countries over their alleged failure to combat forced labor, replacing the expiring 10% global levy imposed by Trump. Trade envoy Jamieson Greer con...
